title: Adversarial reviewer verdict
description: Structured verdict an adversarial reviewer reports per docs/migration/orchestration-plan.md 'Verification pyramid' layer 3 (adversarial review: 20% sample + 100% of XL). One verdict per reviewed connector.

connector.description: Connector name under review; matches the defs/ directory name.

verdict.description: pass iff there are zero blocker-severity findings. A connector may pass with major/minor findings recorded for follow-up; a single blocker forces fail.

findings.description: Every defect found, regardless of verdict (a passing review with only minor findings should still list them).

severity.description: blocker: forces verdict=fail (e.g. schema fidelity mismatch vs live API, secret leaked into a fixture, unjustified escape hatch, undocumented parity deviation). major: should be fixed before merge but doesn't by itself force fail (e.g. a fixture that's technically valid but unrealistic). minor: style/polish, non-blocking.

file.description: Repo-relative path the finding is about.

issue.description: What's wrong and why, grounded in conventions.md or the API's documentation_url.

checked.description: The verification-pyramid layer 3 checklist (orchestration-plan.md 'Verification pyramid' item 3): schema fidelity vs live API docs, fixture realism, escape-hatch justification, secret redaction, conventions adherence.

schema_fidelity_spot_checks.description: Count of stream schemas spot-checked against the vendor's own documentation_url (orchestration-plan.md mandates at least 3 streams when the connector has 3 or more).

write_actions_verified.description: True iff every write action's method/path/required fields were checked against documentation_url (orchestration-plan.md: 'every write action', not a sample).

fixtures_realistic.description: True iff fixtures reflect the real API's recorded response shape (field names, nesting, null-vs-absent behavior) rather than a synthetic-trivial stand-in, per conventions.md section 4.

escape_hatches_justified.description: True iff every hook/Tier-3 escalation reported in the migration result's escape_hatches[] maps to a legitimate trigger per conventions.md sections 1 and 6 (not a workaround for something Tier 1 could express).

secret_scan_clean.description: True iff no secret-shaped literal was found in fixtures or docs.md beyond what connectorgen validate's secret_literal rule / conformance's secret_redaction check already catch (an independent human/reviewer re-check, not merely trusting the automated scan passed).

conventions_adherent.description: True iff naming, x-secret discipline, schema-as-projection, sync-mode derivation, api_surface depth, and docs.md headings all match docs/migration/conventions.md.
